rulings.Case Title: MERCHANTS EXCHANGE BUILDING LLC VS. SUNG CHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT SUNG CHO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The Court has considered the supplemental filings. Defendant's motion to set aside default is denied. Defendant's motion is untimely. Under CCP § 473(b), a motion to set aside must be made within six months of the entry of default. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. CCP § 473(b) and § 473(c)(1)(A) only provide for sanctions when relief from default is granted. This ruling is without prejudice to Defendant's other remedies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: JON GRAY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION AND SAN FRANCISCO CHIEF OF POLICE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Judge Miller does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. Defendants' motion to strike is denied. Pursuant to CCP 472(b), the time to amend runs from the service of the notice of order, unless service is waived in open court. Defendants have not established that Plaintiff waived service of the notice of order. Defendants did not serve the notice of order until September 13, 2013. (See Declaration of Hecimovich in Support of Motion, Exh. A.) Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint was thus timely filed on September 12, 2013. The Court does not address Plaintiff's request, raised in opposition to this motion to strike, to file a further amended complaint. If Plaintiff wishes to amend the Second Amended Complaint, he must file a separate motion requesting that relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: ANDRES MURGUIA VS. ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documetns, Set One.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF ANDRES MURGUIA'S Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documetns, Set One. Denied. Of the six categories in the original motion, only two remain in dispute: (1) Defendant's marketing and advertising materials (Requests Nos. 5-7) and (2) legal complaints and verdicts or judgments against Defendant (Requests Nos. 14 and 15). For Request nos. 5 - 7, Plaintiff has not established that production of documents pertaining to Defendant's representations or advertisements is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP § 2031.310(b)(1).) This action is limited to the handling of one particular claim under on particular policy, and not false advertising. For Request nos. 14 - 15, Plaintiff has not established that production of documents pertaining to legal complaints and verdicts or judgments against Defendant is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The instant discovery is distinguishable from Colonial Life. (Colonial Life & Accident Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1982) 31 Cal.3d 785. In Colonial Life, the discovery at issue was the identities of 35 other claimants who could be called as potential witnesses in support of a "pattern or practice" theory of liability. In contrast, Request nos. 14 - 15 seek discovery of complaints and verdicts/judgments from other lawsuits, which may or may not identify potential witnesses or relevant facts. Furthermore, Request nos. 14 - 15 are overbroad. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: FOODCARE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION (FORMERLY VS. FOODCALC LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT NEEDHAM, LUCY (SUED AS LUCY NEEDHAM A.K.A. LUCY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Granted, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the prayer for attorney fees (FAC, p. 17:20), as to the alter ego allegations (FAC, p. 2:18-19, 3:11-16) and Defendant's personal life (FAC, p. 8:22 - 9:16, Exhibit F). Plaintiff has not pled the statutory or contractual basis for attorney fees. (CCP § 1021.) The alter ego allegations are conclusory. Plaintiff concedes in opposition to the motion that it will stipulate to remove references to Defendant's personal life, and that it will confer with Defendant on this subject. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FOODCARE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION (FORMERLY VS. FOODCALC LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT NEEDHAM, LUCY (SUED AS LUCY NEEDHAM A.K.A. LUCY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the 3rd and 6th causes of action, and overruled as to the 1st, 2nd, 4th and 5th causes of action. For the 3rd cause of action for breach of contract, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege that: (1) there was a contract between the parties; and (2) Plaintiff was a third party beneficiary of the contract. Plaintiff has not pled whether the contract was written, oral or implied by conduct. For the 6th cause of action for violation of Business & Professions Code §17200, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege that Defendant Lucy Needham engaged in any unfair or unlawful business practices; the allegations are conclusory. For the 1st, 2nd, 4th and 5th causes of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each of these causes of action. The request for judicial notice is denied as moot since Defendant withdraws its request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
